game_name: Arcanum: Of Steamworks and Magick Obscura

danger_signals.visual_form: Non-player character model differentiation in isometric view; character stance/animation changes
danger_signals.meaning: Enemy faction or aggressive entity that triggers combat if player enters range

danger_signals.signal: Magic vs Technology incompatibility visual feedback
danger_signals.visual_form: Technological items malfunction indicators; visual critical failure effects in combat
danger_signals.meaning: Character with high magic aptitude equipped with tech items faces increased danger of catastrophic combat failure

danger_signals.visual_form: AI-controlled party member behavioral change (attacking player, leaving party)
danger_signals.meaning: Player conduct violates follower's alignment; party stability at risk

safety_signals.visual_form: NPC dialogue initiation; quest marker indicators; non-hostile stance
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to interact; quest opportunity or information available

safety_signals.visual_form: Environmental cover elements; NPC awareness state indicator
safety_signals.meaning: Player can avoid combat through stealth or dialogue, reducing danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Party member remains in formation; follows player without hostility
safety_signals.meaning: Character actions align with follower morality; party cohesion maintained

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Technologists appear as enemy variants when magic faction is engaged
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Spell effects, magical auras, magical item prevalence in areas

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mages appear as enemy variants when technology faction is engaged
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Firearms, mechanical contraptions, industrial architecture, technological gadgets in environment

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Neither magic nor technology groups are primary enemies; faction flexibility in encounters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mixed magical and technological elements in equipment and surroundings

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Isometric game world displays blend of medieval fantasy architecture (castles, dungeons) with industrial elements (steam mechanisms, mechanical devices, early firearms)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs engage with both magical and technological solutions to problems; quest outcomes vary based on player approach (combat, persuasion, thievery, bribery)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental visual composition shifts between regions: areas show either magical dominance (spell effects, mystical creatures) or technological dominance (industrial structures, mechanical enemies)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character aptitude meter visually represents player commitment to magic or technology path; equipment effectiveness changes based on this meter

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Story progression manifested through environmental changes in affected areas; emergence of otherworldly visual elements signaling the ancient threat's influence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC dialogues reference increasing chaos; quest outcomes relate to prevention or acceleration of this threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High-tech character shows malfunctions (visual glitches, failed ability indicators); high-magic character shows technological equipment as inert or unreliable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat effectiveness fluctuates based on equipment-aptitude alignment; critical failure rates increase with incompatible equipment types

environmental_storytelling.example: Different races (humans, elves, dwarves, gnomes, halflings, orcs, ogres) inhabit distinct environmental zones with architecture reflecting their cultural identity, conveying world hierarchy and territorial control

Areas controlled by technology-aligned factions display mechanical infrastructure (factories, steam vents, electrical elements); magic-aligned areas display mystical flora, magical auras, spell components

environmental_storytelling.technique: Zeppelin crash site as story anchor
environmental_storytelling.example: Initial location (zeppelin wreckage) serves as environmental narrative touchstone explaining player's origin and establishing the ancient power threat
Mainland and three islands within Arcanum world structure suggest territorial divisions and political fragmentation between different factions and races

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Cannot use magic effectively; magical items fail to function; immune to magical effects (beneficial and harmful); tech items function reliably
Spells more effective; magical items more powerful; technological items malfunction with increasing critical failure chance in combat
Leaves party, attacks player, refuses to participate in quests
Initiates dialogue, offers multiple solution paths (combat, persuasion, thievery, bribery), rewards with items/experience/new followers
Engages in combat; combat mode activated (real-time, turn-based, or fast turn-based)
Can be avoided via stealth or diplomacy; player maintains safe distance

notes: Arcanum uses a dual-system visual and mechanical language to communicate the core conflict between magic and technology. The isometric perspective allows clear visibility of environmental faction markers. The Magic/Technology aptitude meter is a primary HUD element communicating world-state alignment. Racial diversity in NPC and enemy design serves faction identification. Combat mode transitions (real-time to turn-based) represent danger escalation. The zeppelin crash establishes narrative stakes visually. Equipment malfunction as a visual/audio feedback system for aptitude mismatch is a key danger signal.
